betterquesting:10.desc:8:
The §6Drill§f is a §6single block§f mining tool that attaches to the bottom of an AR rocket. It auto-mines ores and regolith from the planet's surface as the rocket descends and lands, depositing the items into an adjacent inventory or fluid duct. It is the primary tool for §6asteroid mining§f and §6planetary resource extraction§f.

§6Production Path (drill.json)§f:
- Type: §6minecraft:crafting_shapeless§f
- Input: 1x §6Machine Structure§f (libvulpes:structuremachine), 1x §6Iron Pickaxe§f (minecraft:iron_pickaxe)
- Output: 1x §6Drill§f (advancedrocketry:drill)

The drill has a §3×3 mining area§f centered on its block face. It mines blocks at the same speed as a §6Diamond Pickaxe§f, but it cannot mine blocks with a harvest level above §6diamond§f. The drill draws RF power from the rocket's internal buffer (set per-drill in the rocket assembler).

The drill is a prerequisite for the §6Asteroid Mining Mission§f (chain), the §6Planet Holo Selector§f (chain), and the §6Unmanned Vehicle Assembler§f (chain). It pairs with the §6Specific Impulse§f calculations in the §6Guidance Computer§f (10028) to determine descent speed.

§eTip§f: For asteroid mining, place the drill on the §6underside§f of the rocket frame's center column and add at least one §6Oxygen Vent§f (chain) — most asteroids lack an atmosphere and the drill exhaust will suffocate you without proper suit support.
